nodes.name: 雪山鵪鶉
nodes.summary: 雪山鵪鶉（Anurophasis monorthonyx）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Snow Mountain quail。

nodes.name: 西巴布亚
nodes.summary: 西巴布亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联11种鸟类，包括白腹麻鸭、Snow Mountain quail、Mountain kingfisher等11种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC

nodes.name: 雉科
nodes.summary: 雉科（Phasianidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联179种鸟类，如Udzungwa forest partridge、銹紅林鷓鴣、冠鷓鴣等179种鸟类。

nodes.name: Snow Mountain quail
nodes.summary: The Snow Mountain quail (Synoicus monorthonyx), is a roughly 28-cm-long (11 in), dark brown quail of alpine grasslands. It was formerly considered the only member of the genus Anurophasis, but phylogenetic analysis places it as the sister species to the brown quail (S. ypsilophorus) in the genus Synoicus. It has heavily marked brown plumage, a pale yellow bill, yellow legs and a brown iris. The underparts of the female are whitish and more distinctly barred black than in the male. The Snow Mountain quail is confined to Western New Guinea's highest elevations, the Snow and Star Mountains. This little-known bird is protected only by the remoteness of its habitat, a mostly inaccessible area at altitudes of 3,200–4,200 m (10,500–13,800 ft). The female usually lays up to three, pale brown, dark-spotted eggs in a hollow nest under the edge of a grass tussock. The diet consists mainly of seeds, flowers, leaves, and other vegetable matter. Due to its large range which is largely inaccessible to human encroachment, the Snow Mountain quail is evaluated as "Least Concern" on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species. References External links BirdLife Species Factsheet Red Data Book
